english: III. adj. 1. longed for, earnestly desired. — 2. beloved, v. above. — 3. grieved, tormented frq.; γduṅ-dbyáṅs (gdung dbyangs) a song expressive of longing or of grief, an elegy Mil.; γduṅ-séms (gdung sems) love-longing B., and col.; *ă-ma-la duṅ-sem-čàn °dug* (a ma la dung sem can 'dug) W. he tenderly loves his mother.

beam, piece of timber, má-γduṅ (ma gdung) principal beam, bú-γduṅ (bu gdung) cross-beam; γduṅ-kébs (gdung kebs) beams projecting over the capital of a column Glr. — γduṅ-sgrig (gdung sgrig) a raft Ld. — γduṅ-°débs (gdung 'debs) S.g. pedestal, base(?) — γduṅ-zám (gdung zam) a bridge of timber or of poles. — γduṅ-šiṅ (gdung shing) Sik. fir-tree (Pinus abies).
love, longing Sch., cf. γdú-ba (gdu ba).
Stg.: zás-la (zas la), adj., frugal, temperate?

confidence, assurance, cheerfulness Mil. very frq.; γdeṅ tób-pa (gdeng thob pa) to become confident, to take courage, to be reassured; °či-tse γdeṅ čiaṅ med ('chi tse gdeng ciang med) when dying, he has no confident hope Mil.; mi-°jigs-pai γdeṅ (mi 'jigs pai gdeng) a strong confidence Mil., Thgr.; γdeṅ-tsád (gdeng tshad) id., de-riṅ tsam yaṅ sdód-pai γdeṅ-tsád ma mčís-pas (de ring tsam yang sdod pai gdeng tshad ma mchis pas) not being sure whether his life will be spared for one day more; °či-bród γdeṅ-tsád med ('chi brod gdeng tshad med) without confidence, without any readiness to die Mil.; blo-γdeṅ (blo gdeng) Mil. and col. = γdeṅ (gdeng).

pf. γdeṅs (gdengs), Cs. to threaten, to menace; Sch. to brandish in a menacing way, mtson-γdeṅ (mtshon gdeng) brandishing a weapon Lex.; I also met with: lag γdeṅ-ba (lag gdeng ba) Glr. to raise and move one's hand (in a suppliant manner), cf. dáṅ-ba II. (dang ba), and: bya γšog γdéṅ-pa (bya gshog gdeng pa) a bird with its wings raised and spread Ma.
head and neck of a serpent, sbrúl-gyi (sbrul gyi) Glr.
1. v. γdeṅ-ba (gdeng ba), 2. = γdeṅ (gdeng)(?) *dáṅ-pa-čàn* (dang pa can) W., *ló-deṅ-pa* (lo deng pa) C., deserving or enjoying confidence; faithful, trusty, of servants, husbands, wives etc.

resp. žal-γdoṅ (zhal gdong), 1. face, countenance, γdoṅ skya (gdong skya) a pale face Lt.; γdoṅ-dmar bod-yul (gdong dmar bod yul) the country of the red-faced (more accurately: brown-faced) Tibetans Pth.; γdoṅ-nág (-po) (gdong nag po) 1. a black face; 2. a frowning countenance; γdoṅ-čúṅ (gdong chung) dejected, disheartened, krel-méd γdoṅ-čúṅ mi byed-par (khrel med gdong chung mi byed par) impudent and saucy Glr.; *doṅ-šrán táṅ-čè* (dong shran tang ce) W., *doṅ-šran-te čá-wa* (dong shran te cha wa) Kun., to be forward, bold, brazen-faced; pág-gi γdoṅ-pa (phag gi gdong pa) pig's face, pig's head Sambh.; γdoṅ-bži-pa (gdong bzhi pa) Brahma ('the four-faced'). — 2. surface, superficies, sa-γží (sa gzhi); fore-part, front-part, dóṅ-la (dong la) adv. in front, in advance e.g. to go C.; γdoṅ-γtád (gdong gtad), Ld.: doṅ-stád (dong stad) just opposite; γdoṅ(-la)-°déd-pa (gdong la 'ded pa) to push or press forward, to urge on (a donkey, a coward to the fight), to haul (a culprit before the judge); snáṅ-ba γdoṅ-ded-pa (snang ba gdong ded pa) to pursue one's course regardless of others (both in a good and in a bad sense) Mil.; γdoṅ-pýis (gdong phyis) handkerchief Sch.; *doṅ-si* (dong si) W. complexion, gyur soṅ (gyur song) he has changed colour; to túg-pa (thug pa) and bsú-ba (bsu ba) it is joined pleon.; γdoṅ-lhógs (gdong lhogs) is stated to imply the same as grúm-bu (grum bu) Lt.

= γzód-ma (gzod ma), the beginning, γdód-mai dus (gdod mai dus); γdód-mar (gdod mar) in the beginning, at first Mil.; γdód-kyi(s) (gdod kyis) first, at first, previously, before Mil.; γdód(-ma)-nas (gdod ma nas) from the beginning; γdód-nas dág-pa (gdod nas dag pa) of primitive purity Mil. and elsewh.; da-γdód (da gdod) Lex. prob. = da-γzód (da gzod).

evil spirit, demon, causing diseases etc, stéṅ-°og-gi (steng 'og gi) superior and inferior (spirits), Rahu e.g. is stéṅ-gi γdon (steng gi gdon), an evil spirit of the aërial or heavenly regions; stéṅ-γdon-gyis °či-ba (steng gdon gyis 'chi ba) Glr. = γzas póg-pa (gzas phog pa) to die of epilepsy (W.?), or of apoplexy (Sch.); γdon-čén bčo-lṅá (gdon chen bco lnga), or bčo-brgyád (bco brgyad), frq.; γdón-gyis brláms-pa (gdon gyis brlams pa) Lt., brlábs-pa (brlabs pa) Sch., infatuated or possessed by
